channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Failed Experimental Embalming Caused Decomposition Problems at Pope Pius XII’s 1958 Funeral
item.description: The article describes the widely reported embalming disaster that occurred after the death of Pope Pius XII in October 1958. According to historical accounts, the Pope’s personal physician, Riccardo G...

item.title: Police arrest three following reported shooting outside Sheffield city centre bar
item.description: Three people have been arrested following a police investigation into a reported shooting outside a bar in Sheffield city centre. South Yorkshire Police were called to the One Four One bar on West Str...

item.title: Casey Anthony reemerges publicly as LGBTQ advocate, sparking debate and backlash
item.description: Casey Anthony, who was acquitted in 2011 of charges related to the death of her two-year-old daughter Caylee Anthony, has recently re-entered public discourse with a new online presence that has gener...

item.title: Trump faces internal and external pressure over ongoing Iran peace negotiations
item.description: A report published by Raw Story alleges that President Donald Trump is facing significant political pressure from both domestic and international allies regarding ongoing diplomatic efforts with Iran....

item.title: MBC Shahid Acquires John Achkar’s Arabic Stand-Up Special Filmed at Paris’ L’Olympia
item.description: Middle Eastern streaming platform MBC Shahid has acquired Lebanese comedian John Achkar’s stand-up comedy special “Tryin’” (“Aam Jarrib”), marking a significant moment for Arabic-language comedy on st...

item.title: Ghana organises voluntary evacuation of citizens from South Africa amid rising anti-migrant protests
item.description: Hundreds of Ghanaian nationals living in South Africa have begun registering for voluntary evacuation flights back to Ghana amid growing fears linked to rising anti-migrant protests across several pro...

item.title: Supercars pit lane order confirmed for Darwin Triple Crown after Tasmania Super440
item.description: The Supercars pit lane order for the upcoming Darwin Triple Crown has been confirmed following the results of the Tasmania Super440 round. Triple Eight Race Engineering continues to lead the teams’ ch...

item.title: Saskatoon teacher begins 1,500-km run across Saskatchewan to honour mother and raise money for leukemia research
item.description: Megan Wotherspoon, a teacher and ultramarathon runner from Saskatoon, has started a 1,500-kilometre journey across Saskatchewan in memory of her mother, Louise Tokaruk, who died from leukemia in 1995 ...

item.title: Enhanced Games Launch in Las Vegas Amid Debate Over Performance-Enhancing Drugs in Sports
item.description: The Enhanced Games, a new sports competition allowing athletes to use performance-enhancing drugs under medical supervision, are set to take place in Las Vegas. The privately funded event features mor...

item.title: Supreme Court urges states to ensure menstrual hygiene facilities in schools to prevent girls from discontinuing education
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has strongly emphasised that girls should not be forced to discontinue their education due to the lack of sanitary napkins and proper gender-segregated toilets in schools. A...

item.title: Biography of Mina Mazzini, Legendary Italian Pop Singer Known as “The Tiger of Cremona”
item.description: The article presents a retrospective look at Mina Anna Mazzini, one of Italy’s most celebrated and influential pop singers. Born in 1940, Mina rose to fame in the late 1950s and quickly became a domin...

item.title: Opposition warns of possible withdrawal from assemblies over treatment of Imran Khan
item.description: Mehmood Khan Achakzai, who leads the opposition in Pakistan’s National Assembly and heads the Tehreek Tahaffuz Ayeen-i-Pakistan (TTAP), has warned that the opposition alliance may consider quitting pa...

item.title: Frog-based milk preservation in Russia and Finland and its link to antimicrobial skin compounds
item.description: Before refrigeration became widely available, rural communities in Russia and Finland developed practical methods to keep milk from spoiling. One of the most unusual practices involved placing a live ...

item.title: NYT reports Trump remarks on Sen. Susan Collins could influence 2026 Senate race dynamics
item.description: A New York Times report highlighted how a past public statement by President Donald Trump could become a significant factor in the 2026 U.S. Senate elections. According to the report, Trump’s January ...

item.title: Uganda Confirms Two Additional Ebola Cases Linked to Congo Outbreak
item.description: Ugandan health authorities announced two new Ebola infections on May 25, 2026, increasing the total number of confirmed cases in the country to seven. Officials said all identified cases are connected...

item.title: Protests break out in Central Kenya towns over rising transport fares linked to fuel price increases
item.description: Residents in parts of Central Kenya, including Nyeri, Thika and Nyahururu, took to the streets in protest after a sharp increase in public transport fares. The demonstrations were triggered by rising ...

item.title: IHC issues stay order on eviction of One Constitution Avenue apartment owners
item.description: The Islamabad High Court (IHC) on Monday restrained the authorities from evicting apartment owners of One Constitution Avenue, Islamabad, until further orders, providing temporary relief to sub-lease ...

item.title: Trump links Iran peace deal to expanded Abraham Accords participation by Muslim-majority countries
item.description: Former US President Donald Trump has reportedly tied a potential Iran peace arrangement to wider normalisation of relations between Israel and several Muslim-majority countries under the Abraham Accor...

item.title: HR professional Portia Napaaba honored for leadership in mining, manufacturing, and energy sectors
item.description: Portia Napaaba, a Senior Professional in Human Resources International (SPHRi), has been recognized by the HR Certification Centre (HRCC) for her significant contributions to human resource developmen...

item.title: UCT biomedical research centre granted SAHPRA licence to manufacture medical devices locally
item.description: The University of Cape Town’s Biomedical Engineering Research Centre (BMERC) has achieved a significant milestone after receiving a medical device manufacturing licence from the South African Health P...
